data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-07T00:50:19.774Z</current_datetime>

I have an EF Core 8 app. These two operations are extremely slow because they load all entities into memory:

```csharp
// Operation 1: Deactivate all products not sold in 6 months
var staleProducts = await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ToListAsync();
foreach (var p in staleProducts)
{
    p.IsActive = false;
}
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();

// Operation 2: Delete all audit logs older than 1 year
var oldLogs = await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ToListAsync();
_db.AuditLogs.RemoveRange(oldLogs);
await _db.SaveChangesAsync();
```

There are 500K products and 2M audit logs. How do I make these efficient?

data.content:
Use EF Core 7+'s **`ExecuteUpdateAsync`** and **`ExecuteDeleteAsync`** — they translate to single SQL statements, no entity loading:

```csharp
// Operation 1: bulk UPDATE directly in the database
await _db.Products
    .Where(p => p.LastSoldDate < DateTime.UtcNow.AddMonths(-6))
    .ExecuteUpdateAsync(s => s.SetProperty(p => p.IsActive, false));

// Operation 2: bulk DELETE directly in the database
await _db.AuditLogs
    .Where(l => l.CreatedAt < DateTime.UtcNow.AddYears(-1))
    .ExecuteDeleteAsync();
```

**Why this is faster:**
- Generates a single `UPDATE`/`DELETE` SQL statement — no round-trips per entity
- **Zero entities loaded into memory** (bypasses the change tracker entirely)
- For 500K products and 2M logs, this goes from minutes → seconds

**Caveats:**
- These bypass the change tracker, so any already-tracked entities won't reflect the changes — call `_db.ChangeTracker.Clear()` afterward if needed
- Navigation/cascade deletes defined only in EF (not the DB) won't fire — ensure your DB schema has the cascade rules if needed
- No `SaveChangesAsync()` call required — execution is immediate
data.reasoningText: The user wants to optimize two bulk operations in EF Core 8. The key feature here is `ExecuteUpdateAsync` and `ExecuteDeleteAsync`, which were introduced in EF Core 7 and execute directly on the database without loading entities into memory.
